engagement.name: HubSpot CRM Implementation
engagement.scopeSource: The Milestone Group HubSpot CRM Proposal.docx

phases.name: Contacts & Activity Capture

workstreams.name: CRM Architecture & Setup
tasks: Configure HubSpot portal settings and branding
Set up user roles and permissions for 4 offices (Atlanta, Dallas, Denver, South Florida)
Create team structure matching Milestone org
Configure up to 10 user seats

workstreams.name: Contact & Company Management
tasks: Define custom properties (up to 30) for PE relationship tracking
Configure lifecycle stages for BD pipeline
Build parent-child company associations (PE firm to portfolio companies)
Set up association labels for relationship types
Define persona categories (broker, evangelist, investment partner, lender, competitor)
Configure deduplication strategy and rules

workstreams.name: Email & Communication Setup
tasks: Configure Google Workspace integration (Gmail + Calendar)
Set up email tracking settings per user
Connect inboxes for each user (up to 10)
Configure email domain exclusion rules

workstreams.name: Integration: Google Workspace
tasks: Configure calendar sync for meeting auto-logging
Set up contact sync automation
Validate bidirectional sync across all users
Test meeting capture with calendar events

workstreams.name: Data Migration: Contact Import
tasks: Audit existing contacts from Excel, OneNote, and email exports
Map source fields to HubSpot properties
Cleanse and deduplicate contacts across 3-4 sources
Execute test load with sample batch (500 contacts)
Validate test load: field mapping, associations, dedup accuracy
Execute full migration (estimated 2,000-5,000 contacts)
Post-migration validation and cleanup

workstreams.name: Automations & Workflows
tasks: Build activity capture automation (auto-log emails, calls, meetings)
Configure task reminder triggers for follow-up workflows
Set up auto-contact creation rules from email/meeting interactions
Build domain exclusion rules (filter personal email, spam)
Configure lead routing by office/territory
Build up to 8 automation workflows

successCriteria: All 10 users can log in with correct roles and permissions
Parent-child company hierarchy reflects Milestone portfolio structure
Contacts migrated from all sources with <5% duplicate rate
Email and calendar sync working for all users
Auto-contact creation firing on new email/meeting interactions
Task reminders and follow-up workflows triggering correctly

phases.name: Deal Pipeline & Reporting

workstreams.name: Pipeline & Deal Management
tasks: Build deal pipeline with 4-6 stages (to be defined in scoping call)
Configure required fields per stage (stage gate enforcement)
Set up qualification criteria and deal properties
Configure multi-party deal associations (brokers, lenders, owners per deal)
Test pipeline flow with sample deals

workstreams.name: Reporting & Dashboards
tasks: Build BD activity dashboard (calls, meetings, emails per rep)
Build contact engagement overview dashboard
Build deal pipeline visibility dashboard for executives
Create up to 10 custom reports
Configure scheduled report delivery to stakeholders

successCriteria: Deal pipeline configured with stage gates enforcing required fields
Multi-party associations working (broker + lender + owner linked to deals)
Executive dashboard showing pipeline status, BD activity, and contact metrics
Sample deals flow through pipeline correctly with all associations intact

phases.name: Integrations, Training & Go-Live

workstreams.name: Integration: Fireflies
tasks: Configure Fireflies-to-HubSpot native integration
Set up transcript mapping to CRM contact records
Configure contact matching logic for meeting participants
Validate activity logging rules with test meetings
Document known limitations and edge cases

workstreams.name: Training: Admin
tasks: Deliver admin training session 1: settings, properties, user management
Deliver admin training session 2: workflows, reporting, troubleshooting
Provide admin reference documentation walkthrough

workstreams.name: Training: End Users
tasks: Deliver end-user training session 1: daily CRM usage and contact management
Deliver end-user training session 2: deal pipeline and task management
Deliver end-user training session 3: mobile app setup and on-the-go usage
Accommodate varying tech comfort levels (ages 25-65)

workstreams.name: UAT & Go-Live
tasks: Create UAT test scripts covering all configured features
Conduct UAT session 1 with power users
Conduct UAT session 2 with broader team
Fix bugs identified during UAT
Execute go-live cutover plan
Provide 2-week hypercare support post-launch

workstreams.name: Documentation & SOPs
tasks: Create admin reference guide
Create end-user guide with daily workflows
Create mobile quick-start guide
Document CRM hygiene practices and weekly review process

successCriteria: Fireflies integration auto-logging transcripts to correct contact records
All admins trained and able to manage properties, workflows, and reports
All end users trained across 4 offices including mobile app
UAT complete with all critical bugs resolved
Go-live executed with all users active
Documentation delivered: admin guide, user guide, mobile guide

risks.name: Data Quality Risk
risks.impact: Contacts scattered across Excel, OneNote, and email may contain duplicates and inconsistent formatting, increasing migration effort
risks.mitigation: Conduct data audit before import; deduplicate in staging area; small test loads before full migration; set up HubSpot duplicate detection

risks.name: Adoption Risk
risks.impact: Team members with varying tech comfort (ages 25-65) may resist CRM adoption, reverting to Excel/OneNote habits
risks.mitigation: Start with minimal required fields; automate capture; role-specific training; leadership champions usage; phased rollout builds confidence

risks.name: Scope Creep
risks.impact: Client may request Asana integration, advanced reporting, or deal scoring before Phase 1 is stable
risks.mitigation: Enforce phased approach; document Phase 2/3 features as out of scope for current phase; change order process for additions

risks.name: Stakeholder Availability
risks.impact: Key decision-makers distributed across 4 offices with active deal flow, making scheduling difficult
risks.mitigation: Recurring weekly check-ins; async communication for approvals; one primary POC (Chris Bartlett) for decisions

risks.name: Data Migration Volume
risks.impact: Contact volume described as thousands without exact count; actual volume could increase migration hours
risks.mitigation: Request full contact export by week 2; assess volume before finalizing migration plan; cap initial import if needed

risks.name: Integration Complexity: Fireflies
risks.impact: Fireflies integration may have limitations on transcript format, contact matching, or API rate limits
risks.mitigation: Test integration with sample meetings before go-live; validate contact matching logic; document limitations

risks.name: Licensing Cost Uncertainty
risks.impact: HubSpot tier selection may need adjustment; features assumed in starter may require upgrade
risks.mitigation: Validate feature requirements against tier matrix before purchase; negotiate during HubSpot sales quarter

risks.name: Timeline Risk
risks.impact: Dependencies between contact migration and automation setup could create bottlenecks
risks.mitigation: Sequence data migration before automation config; buffer between phases; avoid parallel dependent workstreams

assumptions: HubSpot Sales Hub Starter or Smart CRM tier (final tier TBD after scoping call)
Up to 10 licensed CRM users across 4 offices
Google Workspace (Gmail + Google Calendar) as primary email/calendar platform
2-3 reporting dashboards with up to 10 custom reports
1 test data load before final migration
Phased rollout: contacts/activity first, deals second, integrations third
exclusions: Salesforce, Zoho, or any CRM other than HubSpot
Advanced deal flow management beyond basic pipeline (deal scoring, weighted pipeline)
Marketing automation (email campaigns, sequences, landing pages)
HubSpot Service Hub or Operations Hub features
Hardware procurement or IT infrastructure changes
clientResponsibilities: Provide complete contact exports from Excel, OneNote, and email by week 2
Designate 1 primary point of contact for decisions and approvals
Ensure stakeholder availability for discovery, UAT, and training sessions
Procure HubSpot licenses before implementation begins
Procure or confirm Fireflies.ai subscription with API access
Provide Google Workspace admin access for email/calendar integration setup
Champion CRM adoption internally -- leadership must set expectations for usage
Complete UAT testing within agreed timeline
Communicate deal stages and qualification criteria during scoping call
